concerns.text: FR-001 requires downloading HEA composition data from open repositories (Materials Project, NIST, Zenodo). The plan.md and research.md explicitly state that no verified URL for HEA compositions exists in the provided 'Verified datasets' block. The plan proposes a fallback to a local file or user-provided URL, but this does not satisfy the FR requirement to 'download... from at least one open repository' automatically. The implementer has no task to fetch the primary data source, and the success criterion (SC-001) cannot be measured if the data is missing.

FR-003 requires filtering the dataset to retain only single-phase alloys at room temperature. The plan relies on the existence of the HEA composition data (see FR-001 concern). Without a guaranteed data source in the plan, the filtering logic has no input data to process, leaving the requirement unbacked by a concrete data acquisition step in the implementation plan.

FR-005 requires evaluating model performance on a random hold-out test set. The plan.md mentions 'evaluates on a [deferred] random hold-out test set' but does not define the split strategy (e.g., 80/20) or the mechanism to ensure the test set is held out during training. While the code structure implies this, the plan lacks an explicit step or contract defining the data split logic required to satisfy the FR.
